name: empty_vec_not_unique
reason: Empty collections are vacuously unique; if the spec entails they are not unique, the definition of seq_is_unique is wrong

name: always_unique
reason: If every input is deemed unique, the function can never detect duplicates, making test_unique trivially true

name: single_element_not_unique
reason: A singleton sequence is trivially unique; if the spec says otherwise, seq_is_unique or abstractify_end_points is misstated

name: duplicates_still_unique
code:
proof fn phi_4_duplicates_still_unique(endpoints: Vec<EndPoint>, i: int, j: int)
    requires
        0 <= i && i < endpoints@.len(),
        0 <= j && j < endpoints@.len(),
        i != j,
        endpoints@[i]@ == endpoints@[j]@,
    ensures
        seq_is_unique(abstractify_end_points(endpoints)),
{
    reveal(seq_is_unique::<AbstractEndPoint>);
}
reason: A sequence with two equal elements at distinct indices is not unique; if the spec entails uniqueness here, duplicate detection is broken

name: nonempty_never_unique
reason: If every non-empty collection is deemed non-unique, the function would always return false for non-empty inputs, making uniqueness checking useless
